issues.claim: engineering layer treats 'abide in Me' (mayi vartate) as untranslatable theistic content
issues.objection: Acceptable scope-honesty. The layer explicitly tags STRETCHED, names the Advaita gloss ('exists in the supreme state of Visnu') and the Vishishtadvaita gloss (uncontracted-knowledge similarity) verbatim. No tradition is misattributed. ekatvam is preserved as a contested term across the three commentators.

notes: BG 6.31's theistic register is honestly tagged STRETCHED and the engineering analog scoped narrowly to substrate-engagement-continuity. The Sanskrit's load-bearing terms (ekatvam, mayi vartate, sarva-bhuta-sthitam, sarvatha vartamano 'pi) are preserved as Sanskrit. The two classical commentators' distinct readings are preserved without conflation. PASS.
